configTemplates.intro: Ready-to-paste Klipper config blocks and macros — start sequences, filament handling, input shaping, bed mesh and more. Copy one and drop it into your printer.cfg (the Config Editor is a good place to paste it).

steps: Filter by category to find a macro or config block.
Read the description and what sections it needs.
Copy the template, paste it into your config, and adjust the values.

what.title: What these are
what.body: A curated set of common Klipper building blocks: gcode_macros (PRINT_START, PAUSE/RESUME, filament load/unload, M600) and config sections ([input_shaper], [bed_mesh], [firmware_retraction], …). They're starting points — read each one and tune it for your machine.

insert.title: Inserting a template
insert.body: Copy a template, then paste it into your printer.cfg — the Config Editor's Raw view is a convenient spot. After saving, a firmware restart applies it. Always review the values first; defaults here are sensible but not tuned to your printer.

sections.title: Required sections
sections.body: Each template lists the config sections it defines or depends on. If a section already exists in your config, merge the keys rather than pasting a duplicate (a duplicate section is rejected at startup).

template.def: A ready-to-paste snippet of Klipper config or macro G-code you copy into printer.cfg.

macro.def: A named command you define in config (e.g. PRINT_START) that runs a sequence of G-code.

section.def: A [bracketed] config block, e.g. [bed_mesh] — the unit Klipper configuration is grouped into.
